Check Item: No hardcoded secrets or API keys
Description: AI models frequently embed example API keys or tokens from training data. Search for patterns like sk- or key= in generated code.

Check Item: AI didn't hallucinate non-existent APIs
Description: Verify every import and function call actually exists in the library version you're using. AI models confidently reference methods that don't exist.

Check Item: All external API calls have error handling
Description: Every fetch/axios/http call needs try-catch with meaningful error responses. AI often generates the happy path only.

Check Item: No SQL injection vectors
Description: Check that all database queries use parameterized queries or ORM methods. AI sometimes generates string concatenation for dynamic queries.

Check Item: Input validation on all user-facing endpoints
Description: Validate and sanitize all inputs. Check for type coercion issues and boundary values.

Check Item: Dependencies actually exist and are maintained
Description: AI may suggest packages that are deprecated, renamed, or entirely fictional. Check npm/PyPI before installing.

Check Item: No unnecessary abstraction layers
Description: AI loves creating AbstractFactoryProviderManagers. If a simple function works, don't accept a class hierarchy.

Check Item: Test coverage for core logic paths
Description: Every function with branching logic needs tests for each branch. Check that tests actually assert meaningful outcomes.

Check Item: Tests verify behavior not implementation
Description: AI-generated tests often test implementation details (mock call counts) instead of actual behavior. Fragile tests are worse than no tests.

Check Item: No console.log or print statements left in production code
Description: AI frequently leaves debug logging in generated code. Replace with proper structured logging.

Check Item: Error messages are helpful and don't leak internals
Description: Error responses to clients should be helpful but not expose stack traces or internal architecture.

Check Item: No N+1 query patterns
Description: Check that database access in loops uses batch queries or eager loading instead of per-iteration queries.

Check Item: Proper TypeScript types — no any or unknown abuse
Description: AI takes shortcuts with 'any' type to avoid compilation errors. Every 'any' should be justified or replaced.

Check Item: Environment-specific values use env vars
Description: URLs, ports, feature flags — anything that changes between environments must come from configuration, not hardcoded.

Check Item: Async/await used correctly — no floating promises
Description: AI sometimes forgets to await async calls or creates promises that are never handled. Check for unhandled rejections.

Check Item: No race conditions in concurrent code
Description: Check that shared state access is properly synchronized. AI rarely considers concurrency issues unless explicitly prompted.

Check Item: Functions are under 50 lines
Description: Long functions are hard to test and maintain. If AI generated a 200-line function, break it up.

Check Item: Variable names are descriptive and consistent
Description: AI sometimes mixes naming conventions (camelCase and snake_case) or uses generic names like 'data', 'result', 'temp'.

Check Item: README/comments match actual behavior
Description: AI-generated documentation can describe intended behavior that doesn't match the actual implementation. Verify comments are accurate.

Check Item: No circular dependencies introduced
Description: Check import chains for cycles. AI generating multiple files simultaneously can create circular references without realizing it.
